industries.name: Targeted Alpha Therapy (Emerging)
industries.share: ~100% of actinium use — entirely oncology research and emerging therapy
uses: Actinium-225 (²²⁵Ac, t½ = 9.9 days) is the most promising alpha-emitting radioisotope for targeted alpha therapy (TAT) — its 10-day half-life is long enough for manufacturing, distribution, and targeting, while its decay chain produces 4 alpha emissions per ²²⁵Ac atom (²²⁵Ac → ²²¹Fr → ²¹⁷At → ²¹³Bi → ²⁰⁹Tl → ²⁰⁹Pb → ²⁰⁹Bi)

²²⁵Ac-PSMA-617 (²²⁵Ac-labeled lutetium PSMA compound): clinical trials showing extraordinary efficacy in heavily pretreated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) — responses seen in patients who progressed on ¹⁷⁷Lu-PSMA therapy, suggesting actinium can treat more resistant disease
²²⁵Ac-DOTATATE for neuroendocrine tumors: the alpha-emitting analog of ¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE (Lutathera) showing promise in patients refractory to PRRT. Multiple Phase I/II clinical trials globally

²²⁵Ac supply is critically constrained — the primary source is decay of uranium-233 stockpiles at Oak Ridge National Laboratory (US) and Dimitrovgrad (Russia). Accelerator production methods (proton bombardment of Ra-226) are being developed to expand supply
Actinium-227 (²²⁷Ac, t½ = 21.8 years) is the parent of ²²³Ra (Xofigo) — used as a generator to produce Ra-223 for Bayer's cancer therapy

companies.name: Novartis (Advanced Accelerator Applications)
companies.use: Leading targeted radioligand therapy company. Conducting multiple clinical trials of ²²⁵Ac-PSMA-617 and ²²⁵Ac-DOTATATE for prostate and neuroendocrine cancers. Major investor in ²²⁵Ac supply chain development including accelerator production partnerships.

companies.name: ORNL (Oak Ridge National Laboratory)
companies.use: Primary US source of actinium-225, extracted from legacy uranium-233 stockpiles in the ORNL research reactor. Supplies ²²⁵Ac to pharmaceutical companies and research institutions for clinical trial drug manufacturing under DOE Isotope Program.

companies.name: SHINE Medical Technologies
companies.use: Developing accelerator-based ²²⁵Ac production by proton irradiation of radium-226 targets — could dramatically expand ²²⁵Ac supply beyond what legacy U-233 decay can provide. Working to establish commercial-scale actinium production for the emerging TAT market.
